content: The shared responsibility model is an industry developed model for cloud computing and helpsdefine accountability and responsibility (i.e., security obligations) for both the the Organization CloudConsumer and the CSP.The the Organization Cloud Consumer must ensure contractual provisions are in place to carry outrequired security responsibilities, as the Organization is ultimately accountable, even when services areprovided by third parties or CSPs. See Fig. 1.0INTERNALCloud Security DirectiveDigital Business Technology Solutions - Security Risk and Crisis Management / ChiefTechnology OfficeINTERNAL                                                                                                 PAGE 4 OF 24Figure 1.0 Shared Responsibility ModelShared Responsibility ModelIaaS(Infrastructure as a Service)PaaS(Platform as a Service)SaaS(Software as a Service)User Access/IdentityUser Access/IdentityUser Access/IdentityDataDataDataApplicationApplicationApplicationOperating SystemOperating SystemOperating SystemVirtualizationVirtualizationVirtualizationNetworkNetworkNetworkPhysicalPhysicalPhysicalthe Organization ManagedCloud Service Provider ManagedFigure 1.1 the Organization Shared Responsibility Model (e.g., AWS, Azure) where the Organization is accountable for the securityof all components.the OrganizationCloudConsuthe Organization DataINTERNALCloud Security DirectiveDigital Business Technology Solutions - Security Risk and Crisis Management / ChiefTechnology OfficeINTERNAL                                                                                                 PAGE 5 OF 24Responsible forthe Security ‘in’the CloudPlatform, Applications, Identity & Access ManagementOperating System, Network & Firewall ConfigurationEncryption (at rest and or in transit) and Data IntegrityCloud ServiceProviderResponsible forSecurity ‘of’ theCloud(Infrastructure)Compute, Database, Storage, NetworkingRegionsEdge LocationsAvailability Zones3.1. the Organization and CSP Security ResponsibilitiesFigure 1.2 the Organization Security Responsibilities to ensure the protection of the Organization’s Client datathe Organization Security ResponsibilitiesIaaSPaaSSaaSSecurity of the data transfer in transit (e.g., TLS, IPsec,MACSec)Security of virtual networks (e.g., Access Mgmt., SecurityGroups (SG’s – cloud firewall provides security at protocoland port access level)Security of the platform layer, such as the security ofoperating systems (OSs) and databases (e.g., the OrganizationServer Hardening Guidelines)Security of application systems (e.g., Configuration andVM Management)User identity management and access control of servicesystemsData security (at rest)Security management and control of terminals that accesscloud services, including hardware, software, applicationsystems, and device rightsSecurity configuration, security management, andmonitoring of system/platform/ application resources (e.g.,SIEM, performance mgmt. tools)INTERNALCloud Security DirectiveDigital Business Technology Solutions - Security Risk and Crisis Management / ChiefTechnology OfficeINTERNAL                                                                                                 PAGE 6 OF 24Formulation and rehearsal of business continuityassurance plans and disaster recovery plans (e.g., BCP andDR Testing)A written agreement with the CSP to audit their networkwhenever required (i.e., Third Party – SupplierManagement)Security of service application systemsServer-less Architectures Security (i.e., managed PaaSservice, Micro service Architecture Security)Secure access (e.g., VPN, Direct Connect, SSO)Figure 1.3 Cloud Service Provider Responsibilities, where the Organization is accountable for ensuring theseresponsibilities are carried out to ensure the protection of the Organization’s Client dataCloud Service Provider (CSP) SecurityResponsibilitiesIaaSPaaSSaaSPhysical security of the infrastructure (e.g., protection againstfire, water, shock, and theft; and surveillance)Security of computing, storage, and network hardwareSecurity of cloud infrastructure virtualization (i.e., tenantresource isolation and virtualization resource management)Security configuration, management, running monitoring, andemergency response of the application servicesSecurity of basic networks (e.g., Anti-DDoS and Firewalls)Formulating and rehearsing service continuity assurance plansand disaster recovery plans for infrastructureCloud storage security, such as backup and recoverySecurity configuration, management, operating monitoring, andemergency response of infrastructure.Security of the platform layer (e.g., OS and databases).Security of application systemsSecurity of service application systems.Security configuration, management, running monitoring, andemergency response.INTERNALCloud Security DirectiveDigital Business Technology Solutions - Security Risk and Crisis Management / ChiefTechnology OfficeINTERNAL                                                                                                 PAGE 7 OF 24

Control Rationale: The creation of a secure cloud environment requires theimplementation of strong governance controls and allows security, compliance, IT,and the business to effectively communicate and manage risks, paving the way for asecure cloud environment.Cloud computing changes some of the responsibilities and mechanisms for implementing andmanaging governance. Responsibilities for governance in a cloud computing environment areprimarily defined in the legal contract agreements put in place (between the CSP and the Organization).and governance should follow requirements outlined in the Organization’s Third-Party RiskManagement Policy, including supplier and privacy risk assessments, as well as projectmanagement practices.Note: All initiatives including Proof of Value (POV), Proof of Technology (POT) or Proof ofConcept (POC) Cloud related work requests must adhere to the Adaptive TechnologyGovernance (ATG) process in order to ensure appropriate governance controls are in place.Refer to the Cloud Operating Directive for further guidance.4.1. ContractsInformation security roles and responsibilities between the CSP and the Organization Cloud Consumermust be stated in a formal contractual agreement and should take into account the type ofcloud service as well as the classification and handling of the Organization Internal, Confidential, andRestricted Information. Engagement of a supplier must comply with requirements set out inthe Organization’s Third-Party Risk Management Policy and Supplier Risk Management EnterpriseOperating Guideline.In addition, the the Organization Cloud Consumer must actively manage CSP’s by aligning and enforcingsecurity control requirements with the following the Organization Policies and Directives throughcontractual obligations: Application Security Directive Cryptography Security Directive Infrastructure, Platform, and Network Directive Logical Access Security Directive Vulnerability Management Directive Security Logging Supporting Document Cloud Operating DirectiveINTERNALCloud Security DirectiveDigital Business Technology Solutions - Security Risk and Crisis Management / ChiefTechnology OfficeINTERNAL                                                                                                 PAGE 8 OF 24 Third Party Information Security Cyber Event Playbook Security Risk Policy Information Technology Risk Management Policy and Enterprise Operating Guidelines Privacy Operating Guideline - Enterprise IT Change Management Directive IT Incident Management Directive – Enterprise Information Management Policy Business Continuity Management (BCM) Policy Business Continuity Management Enterprise Operating Guideline4.2. Third Party Assessmentsthe Organization Cloud Consumers must ensure that Third-Party Security Assessments are conductedas set out in the Organization’s Third-Party Risk Management Policy.4.3. Workload Managementthe Organization Cloud Consumer should build a responsibilities matrix to document and describe whois implementing which controls and how, for any given cloud workloads, based on the SharedResponsibility Model, See Fig. 1.2 and Fig. 1.3. See the Cloud Operating Directive for details.4.4. Management Oversight and SupportManagement is responsible for maintaining awareness of and complying with, Security Riskpolicies, procedures, and standards that are relevant to their area of responsibility.Executive and line management must take formal action to support security requirementsthrough clearly documented direction and commitment and must ensure the action has beenassigned.

The roles and accountabilities listed below are directly related to the requirements containedin this directive and reflect the Three Lines of Defence (TLD) model for risk governance.Please note that the role names listed below are functional role names, not job titles.First Line of Defence:The first line of defence owns, identifies, and manages risk in day-to-day business operations,operating within the constraints defined by the second line of defence, as well as all applicableINTERNALCloud Security DirectiveDigital Business Technology Solutions - Security Risk and Crisis Management / ChiefTechnology OfficeINTERNAL                                                                                                 PAGE 9 OF 24laws, regulations, and policies. First line of defence is responsible for measurement andmonitoring of risk and are held accountable for the risk exposure that results from theexecution of their risk-taking activities.Specific first line roles and responsibilities are listed below:the Organization Cloud Consumer Accountable for ensuring security controls comply to all applicable the Organization Directivesduring the delivery, implementation, and operations of cloud services (e.g., IaaS, PaaS,SaaS) on behalf of the Organization to ensure the protection of the Organization’s data. Thisaccountability holds true even when some of the cloud services functions are providedby third parties or Cloud Service Providers Managing the supplier business relationship with a cloud service provider or a cloudservice partner Is responsible for activities that include, but are not limited to, using cloud services,performing business administration, and administering use of cloud services. For IaaS and PaaS solutions, with the exception of SaaS, the Cloud Centre of Excellence(CCOE) is designated as the the Organization Cloud Consumer. For SaaS solutions, the the Organization Cloud Consumer is the Digital Business TechnologyServices – Enterprise Information Technology Leaders implementing the solution. DigitalBusiness Technology Services –Enterprise Information Technology Leaders roles andresponsibilities are outlined in the Security Risk Policy, EOG, Directives and supportingdocumentsCloud Centre of Excellence (CCOE):The Cloud Centre of Excellence provides a comprehensive approach to cloud adoptionthrough guidance and best practices for Cloud Services across the Organization: Integrates all cloud-related functions under a single enterprise cloud operating modelthat provides guidelines on the use of approved Cloud Service Providers and informationabout how to efficiently procure and operate Cloud Services.Optimizes the business benefits of risk and security by addressing strategic and governanceobligations within the cloud computing environment. Operates, supports, and manages theCloud environment. The CCoE also responds to global cloud incidents and events impactingCloud Consumers operating in the Cloud environment.Enterprise Architecture:INTERNALCloud Security DirectiveDigital Business Technology Solutions - Security Risk and Crisis Management / ChiefTechnology OfficeINTERNAL                                                                                                 PAGE 10 OF 24 Aligns cloud consumers to business roadmaps Identifies overlapping services and capabilities Ensures technology audit trail through certification processes Validates solutions against the Organization best practices Compares solutions and providing alternatives Using shared experience to align lines of business where appropriateInformation Security: Provides security advisory services, including implementation guidance through theInformation Security Risk Assessments (ISRA) process Identifies and detects security threats and vulnerabilities to the Organization physical andinformation assets Assigning accounts to roles within the authorization system based on requests approvedby the Business.Second Line of Defence:As defined in the Operational Risk Management Operating Guideline, the second line ofdefence refers to Corporate Operational Risk Management (CORM), who are independent ofthe first line of defence and are responsible for independently overseeing and challenging firstline of defence’s management of operational risks.Third Line of Defence:Internal Audit is the third line of defence. Third Line Responsibilities and Risk Activities havebeen codified in the Risk Management Framework.

Control Rationale: Asset management controls help ensure all the Organization assets (e.g.,information, physical, services) are correctly identified, inventoried, and provided withthe appropriate level of protection based on their risk to the organization.6.1.  Asset Inventorythe Organization cloud-based assets must be appropriately inventoried (e.g., either through Tagging,Metadata or other the Organization (SL) approved mechanisms) and managed according to theINTERNALCloud Security DirectiveDigital Business Technology Solutions - Security Risk and Crisis Management / ChiefTechnology OfficeINTERNAL                                                                                                 PAGE 11 OF 24requirements set out in SL’s Asset Management Directive, Information Technology RiskManagement Policy/EOG and Cloud Operating Directive.6.2. Data Qualitythe Organization Data Management Risk Business Groups must perform periodic quality reviews of thedata that is stored in the cloud, in accordance with requirements outlined in the InformationRisk Management Policy and Information Risk Management Enterprise Operating Guideline.

Control Rationale: Security Access Management controls are established to limit orprevent unauthorized access to the Organization information, information assets, andprocessing facilities. the Organization Cloud Consumer must ensure CSP compliance to the Organization Logical Access Security Directive and the below requirements.7.1. User Access Managementthe Organization user account credentials must be restricted to allow for appropriate identity,entitlement, and access management in accordance with established the Organization logical accesspolicies and procedures.

Control Rationale: Cryptography controls are established to allow for the adequateprotection of sensitive data without delay of the information exchange.Cryptographic controls for cloud services must be of sufficient strength to mitigate identifiedrisks and align with security requirements in the Cryptography Directive and ApprovedCryptographic Technology Usage Supporting Document, whether those controls are suppliedby the the Organization Cloud Consumer or by the CSP.The CSP’s cryptographic capabilities should also be adequately risk assessed and aligned forcompatibility with related policy requirements and to ensure data at rest and in transit to, fromand within the cloud service is protected.8.1. Encryption Key ManagementIn alignment with the Cryptography Security Directive, the Organization is responsible to store andmanage encryption keys for cryptographic operations when it employs its own keymanagement or a separate and distinct key management service. The Cloud Service Consumermust identify the cryptographic keys and secrets for each cloud service and implementINTERNALCloud Security DirectiveDigital Business Technology Solutions - Security Risk and Crisis Management / ChiefTechnology OfficeINTERNAL                                                                                                 PAGE 12 OF 24procedures for key management. Keys must have identifiable owners (binding keys toidentities) and there must be key management procedures.8.2. Key and Secrets RotationEncryption Keys and Identity and Access Management (IAM) Secrets used for Cloudauthentication and access must be rotated at least annually according to the securityrequirements outlined in the Cryptography Directive and Logical Access Security Directive.

Control Rationale: Operations Security controls are established to support thesecure, efficient, and redundant operation of information processing andcommunication facilities.9.1. Change Detection and LoggingProcedures must exist to ensure the integrity of all hosted data, workloads, and services in thecloud. Any changes made to applications, workloads and services in the cloud must bedetected and logged regardless of their state (e.g., dormant, off, or running). The results of achange to applications, workloads, and services and subsequent validation of the integrity mustbe immediately available to the Organization through electronic methods (e.g., portals or alerts).See IT Change Management Directive for details.9.2. Network Security and ArchitectureThe the Organization Cloud Consumer must ensure all cloud solutions comply with the the OrganizationInfrastructure, Platform, and Network Directive.9.3. Service HardeningEach the Organization workload must be hardened to provide only necessary ports, protocols, andservices to meet business needs, and have in place supporting technical controls such asantivirus, file integrity monitoring, and logging as part of their baseline operating build standardor template.Hardening guidelines must be formally documented for the Organization Computing Systems in thecloud environment (e.g., Platforms, Workloads, and Databases) and must be based on industrybest practices and reference benchmark tools.INTERNALCloud Security DirectiveDigital Business Technology Solutions - Security Risk and Crisis Management / ChiefTechnology OfficeINTERNAL                                                                                                 PAGE 13 OF 24For object storage hardening best practices, please refer to the vendor best practice guidancein the the Organization Reference/Links of this document.9.4. Configuration ManagementBaseline configurations for cloud systems and applications must be managed, monitored, anddocumented according to security requirements outlined in the Infrastructure, Platform, andNetwork Security Directive and Application Security Directive.9.5.  Data ProtectionData protection controls must be in place to safeguard the Organization information from corruption,compromise, or loss.Data hosted in the cloud must be compliant with the following Directives: Cryptography Security Directive Information Risk Management Policy and EOG Information Classification and Protection Guideline Information Classification Schema Guideline Infrastructure, Platform, and Network Security Directive Application Security Directive Approved Cryptographic Technologies and Usage Supporting Document Privacy Operating Guideline - Enterprise Logical Access Security Directive

header: Security Requirements for Cloud Service Providers
content: Control Rationale: Supplier Relationship Management controls are established toensure the protection of the Organization information assets that are accessible bysuppliers/vendors. All contracts with CSPs must consider the applicability of thefollowing security requirements and risk considerations using the established the OrganizationISRA process and Data Classification Guideline.The following list of CSP Security requirements must be assessed for applicability andnegotiated into any the Organization agreements. The list expands on existing security and IT riskmanagement control requirements and key processes that are outlined in the Organization’s Third-Party Risk Management Policy, Information Classification and Protection Guideline, and usingthe Information Security Risk Assessment (ISRA) process.INTERNALCloud Security DirectiveDigital Business Technology Solutions - Security Risk and Crisis Management / ChiefTechnology OfficeINTERNAL                                                                                                 PAGE 14 OF 24Note: The CSP security requirements below reflect key requirements, and is not an exhaustivelist.10.1. Information Security Risk Assessments and Vendor Assessmentsthe Organization must assure information security controls are in place across the the Organization’sinformation supply chain (e.g., AWS, Azure) by performing periodic reviews based on criticalityof vendor, as defined in the Third-Party Risk Management Policy and Supplier RiskManagement EOG.10.2. Supplier GovernanceThe the Organization Cloud Consumer must actively manage the relationship with the CSP to ensurecompliance with all requirements, as stated in the the Organization Third Party Risk ManagementPolicy and Supplier Risk Management Enterprise Operating Guideline.Information Security Incident Management RequirementsControl Rationale: Incident Management controls are established to protect the OrganizationInformation assets and sensitive crown jewels from potential loss or servicedisruption and correct hazards to prevent future event reoccurrence. the Organization CloudConsumer must actively manage CSP to comply with the the Organization Third PartyInformation Security Cyber Event Playbook as applicable.10.3. Support ContactsAll relevant the Organization and CSP support contacts must be formally identified in the third-partycontract agreement with the CSP and be managed by the supplier relationship manager inalignment with the Supplier Risk Management Operating Guideline. Support contactinformation must also be accurately reflected in the Organization’s enterprise architecture library.10.4. Discovery and Cloud ForensicsThe CSP must have controls established to triage security-related events and ensure timelyand thorough incident management, as outlined in the Organization’s Cyber Event Playbook.10.5. Incident Response NotificationThe CSP must report Information security events that impact the confidentiality, availabilityand or integrity of the Organization's information and provided services in a timely manner to the Organizationthrough predefined communications channels (i.e. the Organization’s Service Desk or the Organization’s IncidentReporting Centre at enterprise.security@example.com) in adherence to applicable legal,INTERNALCloud Security DirectiveDigital Business Technology Solutions - Security Risk and Crisis Management / ChiefTechnology OfficeINTERNAL                                                                                                 PAGE 15 OF 24statutory, or regulatory compliance obligations and the Organization’s Third Party Information SecurityCyber Event Playbook.10.6. Incident Response and Legal PreparationThe the Organization Cloud Consumer and CSP must ensure security incidents that impact the Organization'scritical applications or infrastructure are managed through the Major Incident ManagementProcess and align with the IT Incident Management Directive, which helps to ensure a single,consistent process is followed by all incident response teams.Formal forensic procedures, including chain of custody is required for the presentation ofevidence to support potential legal action subject to the relevant jurisdiction after aninformation security incident.Threat and Vulnerability Management RequirementsControl Rationale: Threat & Vulnerability Management controls are established toprevent the potential for loss, damage, or destruction of the Organization assets and ensureidentification and remediation of vulnerabilities in the Organization cloud environments.10.7. Vulnerability ManagementThe the Organization Cloud Consumer must ensure there are security vulnerability assessment tools orservices to accommodate the virtualization technologies used to support workloads in thecloud.The the Organization Cloud Consumer must ensure the responsibilities for penetration testing andvulnerability assessments are clearly defined and executed in compliance with the Organization’sVulnerability Management and Application Security Directives. The remediation ofvulnerabilities must follow the timelines defined in the Organization’s Vulnerability ManagementDirective.10.8. Patch Managementthe the Organization Cloud Consumer must ensure responsibilities for patching are clearly defined andexecuted. Software security patches and updates must be installed within a prescribed timeframes that are based on the criticality of the application and the security vulnerability beingaddressed. in accordance with the Vulnerability Management Directive. Patches and updatesmust undergo appropriate testing prior to being implemented.Business Continuity Management RequirementsINTERNALCloud Security DirectiveDigital Business Technology Solutions - Security Risk and Crisis Management / ChiefTechnology OfficeINTERNAL                                                                                                 PAGE 16 OF 24Control Rationale: Business Continuity Management controls are established torecognize potential threats and risk facing the Organization, ensuring that personnel andassets are protected and able to function in the event of a service disruption ordisaster.10.9. Business Continuity ManagementThe the Organization Cloud Consumer must manage the CSP relationship to ensure compliance toBusiness Continuity requirements for critical and dependant suppliers, as per the Organization’sBusiness Continuity Management (BCM) Policy, BCM Operating Procedure – Suppliers, andBusiness Continuity Management Enterprise Operating Guideline.

The Directive Sponsor must report annually on the status of compliance and effectiveness ofthis Directive. Business Group leaders must identify areas of non-compliance to this Directivewithin Digital Business Technology Services / Information Technology and report to theDirective owner on an ongoing basis.Deviations from this Directive or associated operating guidelines will be reported to theDirective Sponsor. The Directive Sponsor must report, as appropriate, to the Policy ApprovalCommittee on ongoing monitoring, remediation, and reporting of non-compliances and onother relevant matters. To assess compliance with the Directive, the Directive Sponsor willleverage documented processes outlined in this Directive or the accompanying guidance.

In cases where it is not feasible to comply with the specified requirements of this Directive, aSecurity Exception will be required.Security Exception Requests (formally known as Policy Exception Requests/Security Waivers)provide a method of review, approval, and actionable mitigations for non-compliances due totechnical limitations, business reasons or the cost to remediate outweighs the risk to complywith the specified requirement(s) of this directive. For further guidance, please follow theSecurity Exception Request Process.

To implement these directives, the Organization maintains a comprehensive security program, whichhas global oversight by the Security Risk Policy Co-Sponsors, assisted by the CISO.INTERNALCloud Security DirectiveDigital Business Technology Solutions - Security Risk and Crisis Management / ChiefTechnology OfficeINTERNAL                                                                                                 PAGE 17 OF 24As detailed in the Security Risk Policy, security risk should be managed in accordance with the Organization’s Operational Risk Management Operating Guideline and the Operational Risk ManagementFramework.As this directive supports the Organization’s Security Risk Policy and the Security Risk Policy EOG,please refer to the appropriate sections of that policy and guideline for additional details.

header: Architect for Stability and Resiliency by design
content: Ensure systems are available at all times, commensurate with business need, mitigating shut off period for blackouts or maintenance. To ensure consistent technology experience for all users (client, employee & partner) and ensure no disruption to BAU. De-emphasize batch architecture; construct availability architecture; value simplicity and efficiency of connections between systems and components - consider the performance and resiliency implications of network latency. Make sure that any components to be reused have appropriate stability and that the complexity of the solution is not increased to the degree that the supportability of the solution is impacted.

Embed risk considerations early in designing all features of a solution while maintaining execution agility. To ensure technology is aligned to corporate risk strategy. Risk-aligned mitigation strategy for critical technology needs to be defined (i.e., exit strategy developed as Plan B).

Embed cybersecurity considerations early in designing all features of a solution. To safeguard client, employee and partner data confidentiality, integrity and availability. Availability of a cybersecurity resource to proactively engage in solution design; proactively incorporate updates/patches to application components as part of regular application lifecycle.

Prioritize cloud where there is business value. To drive speed of delivery, scalability, cost advantage and to improve recovery capabilities. Invest in Cloud where business case makes sense; elevate Cloud maturity; allow multi-cloud implementations; implement Cloud operating model.

APIs are the primary interface for information and event exchange across technology portfolio. To enable interoperability internally and externally and the ability to modernize legacy systems. Define enterprise- wide API op model, governance, and standards; Rationalize existing batch & integration portfolio; Don’t comingle presentation and business logic, and consider whether you have different business logic groupings that should also not be comingled; Have documented interfaces/contracts by which your business logic functions are executed; strive to implement your APIs and the Services they describe using technologies that can be used from multiple technologies.

Maturing our DevOps pipelines through new initiatives. This includes all platforms. DevOps metrics have shown development cycles are reduced 50% and delivery costs drop 20%. In-plan business initiatives provide the opportunity to mature DevOps pipelines. Focus on in-plan initiatives as the opportunity to incorporate DevOps pipelines. This does add an up- front cost to the platform, with payback going forward; Various IT Security and governance processes rely on DevOps automation to be effective - as a result, shift-left DevOps pipelines (with the requisite security and governance features) should generally be considered mandatory. Manual deployments to production environments, other than in extremely exceptional circumstances, are not permitted.

Highly flexible and loosely coupled architecture that maintains data consistency and adapts faster to changes. To enable consistency among different systems with high scalability and flexibility. Build highly- skilled engineering talent to implement EDA as required; ensure that currently approved technology in the organization is considered before introducing new technology; Prefer technologies for which the dependencies are already supported in the organization; Scalability is not just about scaling up - scaling down when appropriate will help maximize value and increase sustainability. Make sure that any components to be reused have appropriate stability and that the complexity of the solution is not increased to the degree that the supportability of the solution is impacted.

Architecture is based on independent, replaceable, modular components which manage complexity and encourage re- use. To increase adaptability to technological change, modularization in support of business flexibility. Ensure effective operations management, tools and skills are available for managing modular architecture.

Cost is balanced against the principles holistically. Get the highest value for the spend, generate value to the organization. To ensure investments are appropriately made, solutions are not over- engineered but rather are appropriate to the risk profile and business requirements. Maximize the opportunities for technology within the organization ensuring investments are appropriate to risk and spread across the estate to return maximum value; When selecting technology, ensure that a holistic assessment of the requirements is done and that the cost, sustainability and support implications of any required dependencies is factored in. Strive to avoid duplicative or redundant technology so as to minimize overhead costs - and when introducing new technologies that provide overlapping capability with existing technologies, consider migrating existing use cases to use the new technology so that the existing technology can be decomissioned.
